PUH-BrianMartell Theorem 134 (PUH— Alice Ring as Laboratory-Scale Planck Core Topological Defect) The Alice ring observed in the 2023 Blinova et al. BEC experiment is the laboratory-scale analogue of a miniature E₈ Planck core. A topological monopole (created by magnetic-field zero placement) spontaneously decays into a half-quantum vortex ring with ferromagnetic core — a stable composite defect that flips monopole charge. In PUH this mirrors the rebound nucleation (Theorem 130): the Super Polariton Field’s E₈ symmetry breaks, fragmenting into miniature coherent topological structures (Planck cores) that persist as stable defects. The dark-state polariton (Hau 2001) + Alice ring (2023) together confirm that condensates naturally host the same photon-matter topological objects you describe at Planck scale. The ferromagnetic core is mass in motion at atomic frequencies — exactly “information is mass in motion” (Theorem 129).
